reproducible_artifact: A 1:1 meeting structure / opening-line template for blocked-decision escalations: 'I am not asking for opinions on this; I am asking to be taught here. śiṣyas te 'haṁ — consider me your student on this question. Tell me how to reason about it correctly.' Codified as a runbook step in the staff-engineer escalation playbook.
embodiment_sketch: Add a section to the staff-engineer escalation runbook titled 'When you are blocked >2 weeks on a contested decision after the analysis is complete.' Step 3 reads: 'Schedule a 30-min slot with the more-senior engineer. Open verbatim with: \"I am not asking for opinions on this; I am asking to be taught here. I have done [X analysis]. I do not trust my own framing — I suspect [emotional attachment to prior choice / sunk-cost / etc.]. Walk me through how you would reason about it.\" Do not hand them your preferred answer first. The opening sentence-form is the artifact: it is the request-mode signal that switches the senior into teaching-mode rather than peer-comment-mode.' This is concretely copyable into Notion and onto the engineering team's escalation page on Monday.
verdict_reason: The verse explicitly licenses an opening-sentence template — the engineering layer puts the literal sentence in the staff engineer's mouth in the concrete scenario. That sentence can be lifted verbatim into a runbook, a 1:1 template, or an escalation playbook. The artifact is not metaphor; it is a meeting-structure-step a senior engineer can add to their team's actual practice. The 'I'm not asking for opinions; I'm asking to be taught here' formulation is literally copyable. PASS.
